source: PDS Dawn FC2 Ceres HAMO Digital Terrain Model adjusted to height above CERES18D geoid
projection: Simple cylindrical / equirectangular, output rotated to -180..180 longitude

zeroLevel.definition: 0 m is the PDS/JPL CERES18D geoid/ceroid surface, approximated radially from the reference ellipsoid plus geoid height

conversion: shapeRadius = 470000 + dtmHeightMeters; ellipsoidRadius = radius of CERES18D reference oblate ellipsoid at planetocentric latitude; ceroidRadius = ellipsoidRadius + geoidHeightMeters; outputMeters = round(shapeRadius - ceroidRadius).
caveat: The geoid height is treated as a radial offset from the ellipsoid at each planetocentric latitude; this is appropriate for this visualization but not a rigorous geodetic normal-height transformation.
encoding: Signed meters are stored as unsigned 16-bit value = clamp(round(meters) + 32768, 0, 65535). Each 16-character RGB mask runs source bit 15 to bit 0; a 1 copies that source bit into the channel, packed left-to-right into channel bits 7 to 0.
geoidLabelSummary: This file contains a digital map of the Ceres geoid derived from the JPL CERES18D spherical harmonic model of the Ceres gravity field. Each point is the Ceres geoid height in meters above a reference ellipsoid (semi-major-axis = 482.0 km, GM = 62.6273588067 km**3/s**2, flattening = 0.074896, and rotation rate = 0.000192340387006239 rad/s). The JGDWN_CER18D_GEOID_0018 is computed from the CERES18D solution (from degree 2 up to degree 18). The map is produced by Dawn Gravity Team (Alex Konopliv, Ryan Park, Sami Asmar) at JPL.
resampling: Bilinear samples from the 21600x10800 DTM and 360x181 geoid at 1080x540 output pixel centers. DTM was read by HTTP byte ranges; geoid was downloaded locally.
